2019-08-25.title: Israel Says It Struck Iranian ‘Killer Drones’ in Syria
2019-08-25.abstract: The military says Iran was planning to attack Israeli targets with a new kamikaze-style weapon.

2019-08-25.title: Sidney Rittenberg, Idealistic American Aide to Mao Who Evolved to Counsel Capitalists, Dies at 98
2019-08-25.abstract: Once a dedicated Communist, Mr. Rittenberg became disillusioned after 16 years in prison in China and returned to the United States to forge a lucrative career helping companies do business in China.

2019-08-25.title: South Korea Launches Military Exercise for Islets Also Claimed by Japan
2019-08-25.abstract: The exercises are meant to protect South Korea’s claim to a set of islets also claimed by Japan, as ties between the two countries are at their worst in years.

2019-08-25.title: How American Gun Laws Are Fueling Jamaica’s Homicide Crisis
2019-08-25.abstract: Hundreds of thousands of guns sold in the United States vanish because of loose American gun laws. Many reappear on the Caribbean island, turning its streets into battlefields.

2019-08-26.title: As Amazon Burns, Fires in Next-Door Bolivia Also Wreak Havoc
2019-08-26.abstract: The fires have engulfed the country’s largest city in smoke, and are threatening villages, productive farms, and endangered species.

2019-08-26.title: G7 Highlights on Final Day of Meetings: Iran, Climate Change and China Trade
2019-08-26.abstract: President Trump spoke again about the trade war, President Emmanuel Macron of France discussed diplomacy with Iran, and world leaders agreed on Amazon aid.

2019-08-26.title: Indonesia’s Capital Is Sinking, Polluted and Crowded. Its Leader Wants to Build a New One.
2019-08-26.abstract: President Joko Widodo proposed stripping Jakarta of its status as the country’s capital and relocating to the island of Borneo. Construction could begin as early as 2021.

2019-08-26.title: Who Is Killing Wallabies in Northern Australia?
2019-08-26.abstract: With 37 of the animals found dead since Wednesday in the same area near the city of Cairns, foul play is strongly suspected.

2019-08-26.title: Trump Has Just the Place for the Next G7 Meeting: His Own Golf Resort
2019-08-26.abstract: Critics say holding the 2020 gathering at his Doral golf resort would be a blatant and unconstitutional attempt to use the presidency for personal gain.

2019-08-27.title: Australian Writer Yang Hengjun Charged With Espionage in China
2019-08-27.abstract: China’s treatment of Dr. Yang, whose work includes fiction and essays critical of Beijing, has escalated tensions between Australia and China.

2019-08-27.title: Huge Oil Spill in Land of Earthquakes, Tsunamis and Volcanoes
2019-08-27.abstract: The true size of the July spill, which has left an inky stain across 12 miles of shoreline and upended livelihoods in a dozen villages, is still unknown.

2019-08-27.title: India’s Widows, Abused at Home, Have Sought Refuge in This Holy City for Centuries
2019-08-27.abstract: Many of India’s castaway widows wind up in Vrindavan, where for hundreds of years they have begged to survive. But their lives have improved considerably of late, thanks to a government shelter.

2019-08-27.title: In Hong Kong, Life Goes On (Despite the Whiff of Tear Gas)
2019-08-27.abstract: Most Hong Kongers have never seen anything like this summer. But that doesn’t mean the city has come to a halt.

2019-08-27.title: From the Shadows, China’s Communist Party Mobilizes Against Hong Kong Protests
2019-08-27.abstract: In Hong Kong, the party is officially nonexistent, but it is in the vanguard of an increasingly firm pushback against the demonstrations.

2019-08-28.title: Berlin Murder Raises Suspicions of Russian Involvement
2019-08-28.abstract: The well-planned killing of an ex-Chechen rebel has raised concerns that Russia may have deployed an agent to kill a Kremlin foe, a tactic it has used before.

2019-08-28.title: City on Edge: Photographs From Hong Kong’s Summer of Protest
2019-08-28.abstract: Lam Yik Fei, a photographer, has been documenting the city’s biggest political crisis from the start.

2019-08-28.title: China Rejects U.S. Warship’s Visit Amid Trade War Tensions
2019-08-28.abstract: The denial of a port visit to Qingdao came in the same month that two U.S. ships were denied permission to dock in Hong Kong, according to the United States.

2019-08-28.title: Suicide Bombers Hit Hamas Police Checkpoints in Gaza
2019-08-28.abstract: At least three police officers were killed and three civilians wounded, security officials said. Hamas declared a state of emergency.

2019-08-28.title: Boris Johnson’s ‘Explosive’ Move to Get His Way on Brexit: Suspend Parliament
2019-08-28.abstract: Britain’s prime minister asked the queen to suspend Parliament, a move intended to ensure that lawmakers have as little time as possible to challenge a no-deal Brexit. The opposition called it a “coup.”

2019-08-29.title: U.N. Vote Could Clear Path to Cutting Peacekeepers in Southern Lebanon
2019-08-29.abstract: It is a small but significant concession to the United States and Israel, which want U.N. peacekeepers to more aggressively pursue Hezbollah activity near the Israeli border.

2019-08-29.title: Chinese Military Sends New Troops Into Hong Kong
2019-08-29.abstract: The move was described by China as a normal rotation, but given the turmoil in the city, it was being closely watched.

2019-08-29.title: Muslims in New Zealand Say They Have Been Sidelined in Christchurch Inquiry
2019-08-29.abstract: Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern said an inquiry into the massacre would make a priority of consulting with Muslims. But so far, they say they have been shut out.

2019-08-29.title: A Plague of Flies Descends on Karachi: ‘They’re Hounding People’
2019-08-29.abstract: Pakistan’s biggest city had heavy rains, bad drainage and a garbage problem — and now, bugs are everywhere. “There’s no cure,” a shopkeeper said.

2019-08-29.title: Samsung’s Leader at Risk of More Prison Time After Court Rules Against Him
2019-08-29.abstract: South Korea’s Supreme Court found that J.Y. Lee, the company’s de facto leader, had paid more in bribes to the former president than a lower court had ruled.

2019-08-30.title: Hong Kong Arrests Joshua Wong and Other Activists Before Anniversary
2019-08-30.abstract: Plans for a large protest on Saturday were called off after the authorities rejected an appeal from demonstration organizers.

2019-08-30.title: She’s a World-Famous Model. So Why Did an Australian Magazine Get Her Photo Wrong?
2019-08-30.abstract: Adut Akech is a 19-year-old modeling superstar. But after a magazine misidentified her in a photo, she says she is no longer staying quiet about racism in the industry.

2019-08-30.title: Five Years Ago Saturday, a Turning Point for Hong Kong and China
2019-08-30.abstract: Beijing’s August 2014 offer to allow general elections — if it chose the candidates — ignited the Umbrella Movement. Protesters were hoping to mark the anniversary on Saturday.

2019-08-30.title: What’s Next for Brexit? Six Possible Outcomes
2019-08-30.abstract: The Brexit crisis is reaching a crescendo. But the outcome depends not just on Boris Johnson but on his allies and enemies both at home and abroad.

2019-08-30.title: ‘It’s Really Close’: How the Amazon Rainforest Could Self-Destruct
2019-08-30.abstract: Climate change and man-made fires could set off a cycle of self-perpetuating deforestation, scientists warn.

2019-08-31.title: In a Tweet Taunting Iran, Trump Releases an Image Thought to Be Classified
2019-08-31.abstract: The president’s tweet, which included a high-resolution satellite image of the damage, was unusual because Iran had neither acknowledged the accident nor blamed the United States.

2019-08-31.title: Hong Kong Protesters Clash With Police After Defying Ban
2019-08-31.abstract: In the most intense day of clashes since June, the police unleashed tear gas, pepper spray and blue dye against demonstrators who ignored warnings to stay home.

2019-08-31.title: Afghan City Survives Third Taliban Assault, but Loses a Top Defender
2019-08-31.abstract: The war is increasingly fought as much on social media and television as it is on the battlefield. The city of Kunduz survived another siege, but a top spokesman did not.

2019-08-31.title: German Elections Reveal, and Deepen, a New East-West Divide
2019-08-31.abstract: Two key votes this weekend are expected to produce an advance for the far right, and add to a growing sense of legitimacy in the former Communist East.

2019-08-31.title: Britain’s Unwritten Constitution Suddenly Looks Fragile
2019-08-31.abstract: Britons were shocked when Prime Minister Boris Johnson suspended Parliament, defying tradition. But that may be just the start.
